claimText: The Nilgiri Tahr is endemic to the open montane grassland habitats of the Western Ghats.
evidenceExcerpt: Endemic to the open montane grassland habitats of the Western Ghats.
examUse: Statement-based question on species habitat and geographical distribution.

claimText: Eravikulam National Park in Kerala hosts the largest contiguous population of the Nilgiri Tahr.
evidenceExcerpt: Eravikulam National Park in Kerala hosts the largest contiguous population.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on protected areas and species populations.

claimText: The Nilgiri Tahr is the only mountain ungulate found in southern India.
evidenceExcerpt: It is the only mountain ungulate in southern India.
examUse: Statement-based question on unique species traits.
distractorHooks: Multiple mountain ungulates exist in southern India

claimText: The Nilgiri Tahr is mentioned in ancient Tamil literature such as Silappathikaram and Sivakasindamani.
evidenceExcerpt: Mentioned in ancient Tamil literature such as Silappathikaram and Sivakasindamani.
examUse: Statement-based question linking environment with ancient literature.

claimText: The IUCN conservation status of the Nilgiri Tahr is Endangered (EN).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on IUCN status.

claimText: The Nilgiri Tahr is the State animal of Tamil Nadu, which has launched Project Nilgiri Tahr.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation Efforts: Project Nilgiri Tahr of Tamil Nadu. State animal of Tamil Nadu.
examUse: Statement-based question on state symbols and specific conservation projects.
distractorHooks: Project launched by the Central Government
